emails.subject: Mozi Minute: 80/90 Cost Theory
emails.body:
Words I like: The cost of settling is higher than the price of ambition. You just pay it later.

Mozi Money Minute: 80/90 Cost Theory

Most people think going from 80% to 90% margins is just a 10% improvement (or 12.5% if you're good at math). But neither are right for this point. It's actually a 100% efficiency gain.

Here's the math: At 80% margins, you spend $20 to make $100. At 90% margins, you spend $10 to make $100. That means your cost per sale is HALF. Let me show you why this matters.

Ex: Two businesses both making $1M. One at 80% margins spends $200k on costs. One at 90% margins spends $100k. Same revenue, but the 90% business has an extra $100k to reinvest in growth. Or better yet - profit. This compounds over time.

To put this in perspective, to take a business from 80% gross margins to 90% gross margins through price increases alone…you'd have to DOUBLE your price to get that same efficiency. It's that big of a deal. I OBSESS over this.

If you don't think you can raise prices, decrease costs by: renegotiating vendor terms, buying in more bulk, buying your vendors (vertically integrating), increasing your client to delivery ratio, switching from recurring services to on demand, adding tech to expand team efficiency, and offshoring talent. Just to name a few.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Want to multiply your profits to scale? We just opened spots for our next Scaling Workshop in Vegas on Feb 3/4th. Our last workshop had 25% of attendees doing $5M+ in revenue - we're selective about who we let in to keep the room quality high.

Margins matter.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Minute: How I Price Upsells
emails.body:
Words I like: A lot of poor people stay poor because they're too cheap to get rich.

Mozi Money Minute: Pricing Upsells

I see tons of businesses with pricing tiers like: $100/mo, $120/mo, $140/mo. Oftentimes, the buying behavior is almost identical at each of these prices. This leaves a lot of money on the table. Instead, when I price upsells, I try to at least double revenue with each new offering tier. After all, if i'm gonna go through the hassle of having a second tier, I wanna (at least) double what I make. Here's how I price them. It's all based on the fractal nature of customers with 20% of customers having 5x the buying power of the other 80%. So I price my upsells accordingly.

Let me explain…

If you get 1 out of 5 customers to buy something for 5x the price, you double your revenue (and more than double profit, since you don't have to repay the cost of getting a customer - and - your cost of delivery are typically lower - relative to the higher price).

Ex 1:

100 customers at $100 = $10,000.

Then you upsell +20 at $500 ($10,000) = $20,000.

But here's where it gets crazy: this pattern is fractal. It repeats. So you can keep the party going (as long as you have the operational prowess to keep up with delivery).

Ex 2:

Take those 20 premium customers. Offer them a 5x tier ($2,500). If 20% take it (4), boom - another +$10,000 - aka - these two upsells tripled our business (while only selling 24 more units).

Real life:

We did this with a service business. Started at $500. Added a $2k tier (20% bought). Then, added $10k tier (20% of those converted). Revenue tripled but profit went up WAY more than that (you wouldn't even believe me if I told you). Think about it. Same marketing spend. Same team size. Just more zeros. The math works at every level because premium clients want premium options.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Want more revenue multiplication tactics? We just opened another Scaling Workshop date in Vegas on Jan 27/28th. Last month's attendees averaged $2.9M in revenue - we turn away 65% of applicants to keep the room quality high.

Add zeros, watch your profit grow.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Minute: How To Kill The Price Question
emails.body:
Words I like: The reason it's taking so long is because you're in a rush. Big things don't happen on small timelines.

Mozi Money Minute: Kill The Price Question

Every time someone asks for price on a call, and a sales rep answers with a number, an angel loses its wings. I've watched a lot of inbound calls in my life. For brick & mortar especially, prospects often ask for price in the first minute. On calls where the rep answers with a number... close rate plummets. Here's the fix. It's so simple, the only thing simpler is NOT training your team on saying it.

When they ask "How much is it?" You say: "It depends on your goal - what made you reach out today?" Then shut up. Let them talk. They'll tell you exactly what they need to hear to justify the price later. That's it.

Example: Consultancy charging $3,000/mo.

Old way - prospect asks price, rep says "$3k", prospect says "too expensive," call dies.

New way:

Prospect: "What's your rate?"

Rep: "It depends on your goal - what made you reach out today?"

Prospect: "We're losing $20k/mo on bad ad spend"

Rep: [asks more questions, finds total waste is $247k/year]

Rep: "Investment is $3k/mo, so you'll save about $7 for every $1 spent with us"

I gave this to a business owner a few weeks back. Close rate jumped in two weeks. No price change. Moral: Price without context is always too expensive. Give them the context first.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Small hinges open big doors. If you'd like the same tweaks we use that help our portfolio companies grow much faster than the market, apply to see if you qualify for a workshop. We just opened up a new one on Jan 27/28. Last month's attendees averaged $2.9M in revenue - we turn away ~65% of applicants to keep the room quality high.

Frame first, price second,

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Orders of Magnitude
emails.body:
Words I like: If you have < $10,000 saved up, don't buy crypto. Invest in the ability to make money. Then, you can restart the game as many times as you want.

Mozi Money Minute: Order of Magnitude

A lot of small businesses stay small because they play small. They think in increments rather than orders of magnitude. And to be clear - 10% improvements when you can get them are great. But there are diminishing returns. You can only get to 100%. In other words, you can't split test your way to a 10x. Sometimes, you gotta do something different.

Think about it like this…

You can't 100x your close rate, but you can 100x your traffic. And oftentimes, small businesses get so stuck in "optimization obsession", they miss the bigger picture.

Ex:

- Incremental: You improve your optin rate from 20% to 22%

- Order of magnitude: You make a promotion that generates 10X more leads

So the magic growth question to ask is: What would it take for us to 10x our__________________?

And the answer to that question is sometimes expensive, sometimes delayed, but almost always clear. Then, in answering that question, you then think "is there something else I could do for the same cost that could grow us even more?" And if the answer is no, you can ditch your optimization obsession, and make that one big move instead.

This is a bit of a more "strategic" email, but it was top of mind from business conversations I had this week. Sometimes the best move isn't doing what you do better, but doing better with what you move.

Reminder: 10x isn't 10% better - it's 1000% better. Invest accordingly.

As promised, money in under a minute.

A single 10x for any reasonable sized business can change your life forever. We've done it with our portfolio companies many times. If you'd like us to help you think through the 10x version of your business, and how to actually make it happen (from people who do this everyday), apply to see if you qualify for a workshop. We just opened up a new one on Jan 27/28. Last month's attendees averaged $2.9M in revenue - we turn away ~65% of applicants to keep the room quality high.

Actually write down the answer to the "What would it take?" question. It may change your life.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: My biggest split test winner of all time
emails.body:
Words I like: Stop crying. It's not happening as fast as you want because you're not as good as you think you are.

Mozi Money Minute: My biggest split test winner of all time

I have run probably 500-1000 split tests in my life. A lot of businesses can unlock 10-20-30-40% increases in revenue with little effort by running A/B tests on different parts of their funnel. And yet, most don't. It's usually because they either don't do it, or they test the wrong stuff. So let me give you my process for CRO (conversion rate optimization):

- Test in order of greatest conversion loss
- If unsure, test front to back (typically the smallest numbers are at the front of the funnel and make the biggest differences in total throughput)
- Test minimum one thing per week per funnel.
- Keep tests separate.
- Don't test more than one thing at a time (unless your existing thing totally sucks).

Now that we've established that, the largest gains I've ever had… happened split testing something I hear almost no one talk about: their tools/integrations.

As much as you measure different tools based on their feature set, one stat which few track (and they don't report) is the conversion rate of the tool itself. Think scheduler integrations, form integrations, video integrations, page builders…the stuff you actually build your business on. It takes a *little* more work to split test them…BUT…IT. IS. SO. WORTH. IT.

If you have 3-4 comparable softwares that are client facing and in the conversion process - TEST THEM before committing. It may be one of the most valuable things you ever do. (my biggest one had a true 45% increase in revenue…to a 2 year existing split test winner).

I put it off because it was a pain…but now…the only thing more painful is realizing how much money I would've made having tested it two years ago.

As promised, money in under a minute.

We just opened up a new one on Jan 20 & 21st. Last month's attendees averaged $2.9M in revenue - we turn away ~65% of applicants to keep the room quality high.

Actually test this,

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Minute: BAMFAM
emails.body:
Words I like: Most businesses obsess over getting more leads when they haven't even fixed what happens when the phone rings.

Mozi Money Minute: BAMFAM

We bought an insurance company over a year ago. The biggest constraint was prospects would drop off between first call & second call. And between those calls the reps would send them the plans they best qualified for. Lots of work. Low return. We made one change: BAMFAM. Which, I got from my friend Sharran Srivatsaa (President of $REAX). It stands for Book-A-Meeting-From-A-Meeting.

Here's how it works: A prospect should never be in no man's land. You CANNOT get off the call with "we'll offline about when a good time is." or "yea Ill follow up and propose some times." You're both there. Right now. Pull up your EFFING calendar. Pick a gosh darn time. If you can't close them on a time when you're right in front of you, you CERTAINLY aren't going to close them later when the pain and problem are in their rear view mirror.

We call it "BAMFAM as a way of life" internal to the team. Someone being dodgy about their next call IS an objection. Handle it. And your sales will thank you.

Result: People stopped falling off in the sales process. The insurance company sales increased. Go figure. And the world was made a better place with a better business.

That's it. No fancy tech. No complicated training. Just a checklist and a script that turns "maybe later" into "see you thursday."

As promised, money in under a minute.

If you'd like our help figuring out what those missing links are in your business that dramatically increase sales/revenue/profit and all other amazing business things, I've got good news. We just opened up our next Scaling Workshop Date on Jan 6th & 7th.

**Last month's room averaged $2.8M in revenue, and we had to turn away 70% of applicants to keep the quality high.

Train your team on BAMFAM. Don't leave money on the table.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Minute: More or better?
emails.body:
Words I like: How to become an expert: Do more repetitions than anyone else in a narrow field.

Mozi Money Minute: More or better?

Without doing anything new, any business can drive growth by doing more of what they're doing, or do what they're currently doing better. Duh. The question is, which one should you focus on?

Good news: there's a math answer. When the expected return from the incremental effort of adding more is less than the incremental effort of doing better, you focus on better. When it's the opposite, you focus on more.

Example Scenario #1: Let's say you have one outbound sales rep. That rep brings you 1 deal a week. You have two options:

1) Improve that guy's closing (expected return: +20% to close rate).

2) Hire a second rep (expected return 100% increase in sales - aka - sales double).

Both cost about the same amount of time. So which do you do? Probably #2 - you just need more reps.

Example Scenario #2: You now have twenty reps bringing you one deal per week. You consider two growth options:

1) Improve the teams closing (+20%)

2) Hire and train 4 more reps (+20%)

So which do you do? Probably #2. It'll probably take less effort to train the team to go from say 25% to 30% close rate than to recruit, hire and train four more guys. At some point though, your team's close rate may approach the upper end of what's possible. At that point, you might not believe you can squeeze out another 20% improvement team wide. So, you'd switch to getting more reps again.

This is how you can figure out the right decision to grow.

Alex

Fyi - next open workshop date is: Jan 6/7th.

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: 50% Conversion Rate Boost
emails.body:
Words I like: If you can't sit still, ignore notifications, and focus on one task for eight hours straight, never expect to build something great.

Mozi Money Minute: 50% Conversion Rate Boost

There are very few things that always work. But, this tactic is about as close as it gets. I once ran all my emails and web pages through this process and got a permanent 50% boost.

Here's how it works: Run every script you write. Every webinar. Every email…through a reading level calculator. It'll tell you a grade. Then, keep correcting it until it's below 3rd grade. I use Hemingway App. Google it. It's free and I have no affiliation.

"Below 3rd grade" usually means: Simpler words. Shorter sentences. Fewer adverbs. No passive language. No excess words.

If you want to take this to the next level, actually write inside of Hemingway. This gives you real time feedback and makes you a better writer that much faster. It was one of the things that transformed my writing.

To be clear: Simplifying language doesn't mean you attract dumb people. It means you make it easier for higher level people, and you make it possible for lower level people.

End result: more people understand you and buy your stuff.

This tactic came up when I spoke to a business struggling with sales. I could tell by the way the founder talked about his product, his language was his problem. After he changed, his business grew. Yours may too.

If you'd like my team to help you find and remove the growth limiters on your business...we just released seats for a new workshop at our HQ on Jan 6/7th.

Keep it simple. You'll sell more.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: What Who When
emails.body:
Words I Like: Staying focused as an entrepreneur is like staying sober as an addict. It's not a one time victory. You have to fight it all day, everyday. Then, wake up & do it again tomorrow.

Mozi Money Minute: WHAT WHO WHEN

I just took over a team recently. I haven't managed day to day stuff in a while, and it's been great. I was talking to one of the leaders on the team this morning and asked him what he's taken from those meetings to his own team. And he replied - WHAT WHO WHEN. It's a framework I use to get stuff done way faster with teams.

Here's how it works.

- Every idea we go through to solve a problem, if we decide it's a priority (WHAT) I say…"Great…WHO is gonna own it?" Then, I say "great…how many hours will that take to do? (not: what day can you get it done by). Then, I ask, "What stops you from doing it in the next X hours?" And if they say something less important, I make that new thing their priority and remove the less important thing. Then I say "Great. Then let's have that done by X hours from now (WHEN)." If the other thing they say is more important, I ask again how many hours it'll take, then either staple my priority on the back end, or push it to the next day.
- Then, we move onto the next item. Once we figure out the WHAT - WHO - WHEN, for the next item, I recap the first and the second WHO WHAT WHENS, then move onto the third. I keep doing this throughout the meeting. So everyone has heard WHO WHAT WHEN 3-5x by the end of the meeting. Then I send a slack recap.

There are many complex meeting systems. I never stick with them. But this has worked well for me. Keeps things simple.

If you want more tactical help scaling your business, we go over management practices to scale people at our scaling workshop. If you want to see if your business qualifies, go here.

Stay wise, prioritize.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Solve for compounding
emails.body:
Words I Like: If you want better friends, get used to seeing them less. Better friends are busy friends.

Mozi Money Minute: Solve For Compounding

As soon as you learn how to market and sell, you can become a millionaire, even a decamillionaire. But to become a centimillionaire and beyond, you need something else. You need to have all that work marketing and selling stick. You need it to stack. So that the size of your company is relative to the sales you do over a decade, not just what you sold last month.

There are only two ways to do this:

- Make something people never stop buying: either a subscription (Netflix) recurring revenue, or something people come back to again and again on a predictable basis (Coca-cola) - aka re-occurring revenue.
- Build a network of people who never stop selling. Think real estate brokerages. The people buying houses doesn't recur per se, but recruiting agents who regularly sell X houses per month is the compounding component of the business. If you have a physical product, all your nodes of distribution (ie. affiliates or retail stores) become the people that stack over time.

You need to solve for one. If you don't, you'll be stuck every month having to live hand to mouth. And you'll always be one bad advertising campaign or domain ban away from losing everything. No fun.

"Sometimes you gotta go slow to go fast." It would be better to spend two years finding something that sticks than spend two years growing revenue through marketing, only to hit a plateau you can't breakthrough.

If you want our help figuring out a compounding vehicle for your business (we've designed them for loads), we just opened up a new date for our next workshop. You can grab a seat here.

Otherwise, slay the day.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: 4 Part Persuasion
emails.body:
Words I like: If you could only pick one skill in business, it would be to get other people to do stuff for you. Because if you only had that, you could get everything else done.

Mozi Money Minute: 4 Part Persuasion

This is so simple, no one actually does it. When you want to persuade someone, you want to do four things:

- Add good stuff they get for taking the desired action
- Take away bad stuff for taking the desired action
- Add bad stuff if they do nothing (or something different)
- Take away good stuff if they do nothing (or something different)

Bonus persuasion points: make 1-4 happen immediately upon them making the decision. And to be clear when I say "add bad" or "Add good" etc, i mean make them aware of

I use this 4-part setup as my mental checklist when I try to persuade people to buy stuff, or take the next step. Applies anywhere you ask people to do things.

Ex: Desired action→come to a workshop so we can help you scale (and meet you)

- Good stuff you get: Personalized tactics from my team to immediately grow your business.
- Remove bad: No more uncertainty for next steps. Know exactly what to do next to scale.
- Add bad/remove good: Alternatively, stay on your exact course, which may end up being the wrong way to grow. Ex: The fastest path to $1M isn't the fastest path to $10M. We can help you avoid that pain by building it right the first time (because we've already made the mistakes so you don't have to).

As promised, money in under a minute.

Btw - if you want us to help you apply these types of frameworks to your business we just released two new workshop dates.

May your desire be greater than that which stands in your way.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Give away better stuff *to better people
emails.body:
Words I like: People worry too much about if it's scalable and not enough about whether it's valuable.

Mozi Money Minute: Give away better stuff to better people

Most businesses could see *instant* 2-3x increases in lead flow by simply giving away something with real cost for free. Unless you have an established reputation, a free PDF has low perceived value. So, raise the perceived value by giving stuff away with a REAL value. But…do it with a concurrent increase in qualifications. So only customers who are perfect qualify for the hard cost giveaway. Ex: If you make over $1M per year, I will spend 5 hours with you 1 on 1 to [solve problem you have]. Guess how easy it is to sell someone an implementation after you spent 5 hours showing them how much work its gonna be to actually solve their problem. Answer: not hard. Guess how many more leads you get: a lot. Give away more, but qualify.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Missing even one tiny tactic like this can slow your growth for years. If you want help scaling [and applying tactics like this in your business], apply for a seat at our next Scaling Workshop at our HQ in Vegas on Dec 18/19.

If you want more people to be interested in your stuff, make it more interesting.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: It’s a bad deal silly
emails.body:
Words I like: Either sell or don't sell. No half measures.

Mozi Money Minute: It's a bad deal silly

Business owners everyday tell me they can't seem to get other businesses to refer them to customers. They think a 20% referral fee will get someone STOKED to send you their hard earned customer to be: 1) disappointed in your services 2) stolen from them 3) without any referrals back. No sh*t they don't want to. If you really want to get referrals, give them a service you offer to sell as their own for whatever they want to charge for it. Yes, that means you eat your hard costs. But that's still usually cheaper than getting a new customer. Ex: You sell accounting. Create a tax lookback product. Give it to them to sell for $5000. Charge $0. Let them keep all of it. You do the work then ask the customer "do you want us to help you look at other years also?...and we can make sure this year you get even more deductions." Reverse engineer your offer from the partner, not from your pocketbook. Then just figure out the upsell to make it work financially for you. THAT is how you get people STOKED to refer.

As promised, money in under a minute.

One good referral partner can explode your profits. If you want personalized tactical help (like this), we opened up spots for our next Scaling Workshop at our HQ in Vegas on Dec 18/19.

Sometimes, you just need to make them a better deal.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Double weeks
emails.body:
Words I like: When it's easy, do more. When it's hard do different.

Mozi Money Minute: Double Weeks

My marketing department felt slow.

So, without them knowing, I implemented double weeks.

Basically, instead of having a weekly meeting. I have two (or more) meetings every week.

This gives me two deadlines (or more) to drive things towards.

If my team gets two weeks (or more) done every week, we'll move faster than a team that only gets one.

Consider using 'double weeks' in a department that feels slow.

- Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: 5 horsemen of stagnation
emails.body:
Words: You only need to get rich once.

Mozi Money Minute: 5 Horsemen of Stagnation

There are many tactics that limit growth, but often it's one 'rock & hard place' scenario that keeps entrepreneurs frozen in place for years.

Here are the top 5:

1. Serve too many avatars
You try to be everything to everyone, so your marketing speaks to no one. Fix: Narrow your focus.

2. Underpriced
You're afraid to raise prices because you might lose customers. Fix: Raise prices and deliver more value.

3. Overcompensated
Your early employees got great deals that no longer make sense. Fix: Hire new people at market rate with new comp structures.

4. Overextended
You grew too fast and now have too many commitments. Fix: Cut what's not working and focus.

5. More than one business
You're splitting attention across multiple ventures. Fix: Pick one and go all in.

The fix for all of these is the same: make the hard decision you've been avoiding.

- Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Retention incentives
emails.body:
Words: You can only create a compounding business two ways: get people to buy more, or get them to stay longer. Better yet, do both.

Mozi Money Minute: Retention Incentives

Give people two subscription incentives.

First, give them the incentive to subscribe (duh).

Then, give them a second incentive to stay.

When: just before your churn point.

If most people cancel at month 3, give them something special at month 2.

Examples:
- Loyalty pricing that kicks in after X months
- Bonus content or features unlocked over time
- Community access that deepens with tenure
- Recognition or status for long-term members

The second incentive doesn't need to cost you much. It just needs to give them a reason to stick around past the danger zone.

Stack enough of these and you build a business that compounds.

- Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Retention incentives
emails.body:
Words: You can only create a compounding business two ways: you sell stuff people never stop buying or you have people who never stop selling.

Mozi Money Minute: Retention Incentives

Give people two subscription incentives. First, give them the incentive to subscribe (duh). Then, give them a second incentive to stay.

When: just before your churn point. Ex: Your avg subscription lasts 6 months. Put your incentive at month 4 or 5. Remind them at 2 and 1 month before the incentive that it's coming (it'll get even more to stick).

What to give: Permanent incentives (lifetime upgrades, status, or savings) get more long term stick but cost you more. One time incentives will get people to stay to that point, then churn immediately after. But, they cost less. But, if you get every customer to go from 6 months to 8 months...that's a 33% increase in revenue (and likely a 2x+ increase in profit). Still worth it.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Please use this email to make money,

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: 5x boost conversion - VSL
emails.body:
Words I like: Assume the audience has no idea who you are, what you do, how it works, they're in a rush, and they have a 3rd grade education.

Mozi Money Minute: 80% drop in conversion...then a 5x

We recently had one of the top converting communities on Skool drop their conversion by 80%. What they did: removed their video on the about page thinking "people are in too big a rush to watch a video." They were wrong, obviously. They put it back up and conversions returned.

This worked for us too. Seeing how powerful the video was (almost nothing else affects conversions 5x), I went to work on trying to beat our control video at Skool.com/games. We ran 100,000 clicks to the page with a new video and got a statistically significant 20% boost in page conversions.

Moral: most people don't watch the video, read the testimonials, or the FAQs. But...THE BUYERS DO. Make your page for the buyers, not everyone. And the buyers will reward you. Put the video back up. Add the FAQs. The right people read them. You need friction to make a fire. And you need to light one to get a buyer.

That is all. As promised, under a minute.

Slay the day,

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Min: Belief Breaking Formula
emails.body:
My favorite quote of all time: "We question all of our beliefs except for those we truly believe. Those, we never think to question."

Mozi Money Minute: Belief Breaking Formula

People talk about breaking the beliefs of your prospect all the time. But no one talks about how to do it. This is the four step framework I use. Worth teaching your sales team when you need to confront a prospect with poor beliefs.

- Say their wrong belief from the first person (so it's not offensive): "I used to think that getting lawncare services was a luxury only the rich could afford."
- Say why it's wrong: "It takes me half a day to do it myself, then I'm exhausted."
- Say what's right: "Then I realized if I make more than a lawncare worker, then it's actually cheaper for me to let someone else do it, and work my main job in the meantime."
- Proof: "John went from dreading his Sundays (when he used to take care of the yard) to working for a couple hours then spending the rest of the time with his family. He no longer keeps up with the Joneses. He IS the Joneses."

If selling 1 on 1 - Repeat with an avatar as similar to the person you're talking to as possible.

If selling 1 to many - add different avatars to represent each avatar you sell to.

That is all. As promised, under a minute.

Slay the day,

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Make testimonials that convert
emails.body:
Perspective:

Rich people buy time.

Poor people buy stuff.

Ambitious people buy skills.

Lazy people buy distraction.

Mozi Money Minute: Make testimonials that convert

Turning testimonials into ads is very profitable, but often, very tough. Main reason: people suck at making testimonials. After reviewing all the testimonials, and what separated the best from the rest - there was one thing the top 4 of all time had in common - a pain based hook. Top 4 all time:

- We were two months away from shutting our doors...
- We were barely making enough to survive...
- I paid payroll, rent, and I'd finish the money with $0 profit..
- I pay all my expenses every month, work all day, only to end up with $0 profit...

So - next time you record a testimonial - don't ask them how life was before they started working with you. Ask them for their worst moment. Moments are what make the hooks. Details are what make them powerful.

Stay rich,

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money: 391% Sales Increase with One Change
emails.body:
Words I like: Money loves speed. Wealth loves time. Poverty loves indecision.

Mozi Money Minute: Speed to Contact

I believe in this so much I almost started a fund of buying businesses to implement this ONE tactic. It's simple to understand - hard to do - but you can do it in a few minutes. Call leads within 60 sec of them opting in. Harvard Business Review published a study that demonstrated an AVERAGE of 391% increase in sales. That means you can do nothing else besides call your leads faster, and you could unlock a 4x in your business. Hard to imagine anything else that will do that for your business today. And if you don't have the sales staff to handle that...hire them. You can afford to pay for them with the THREE HUNDRED AND NINETY ONE PERCENT increase in sales.

That is all. I kept this under a minute.

Otherwise, keep crushing.

Alex

emails.subject: Mozi Money: Easy 21% Profit Increase For Recurring Businesses
emails.body:
Words I like: "If you grow your business without fixing your problems, you grow your problems instead."

Mozi Money Minute - Annual Renewal Fees

If you have a membership or recurring revenue model, and keep people over a year, a great profit tactic is to add in an annual renewal fee. It's a one time annual fee on top of the monthly. It doesn't interfere with front end sales because it's a year away. And if someone stays a year, they're unlikely to leave. The fee is usually the price of 1-3 months. By adding this in, you add 8%-24% to your revenue without incurring any costs. In a business with 20% margins this could add 50% to your profit. This also allows you to keep your advertised (and sold) monthly rate lower, while still having a higher blended rate.

For example, if I charge $39/mo, I might make my annual renewal $99. This takes my annual revenue per customer from $468 to $567 (a 21% increase). This makes my effective monthly rate $47/mo rather than $39/mo. So I get the sales (and churn) of $39 price point but the revenue and profit of a $47 price point. Effective. Use it well.

See you soon,

Alex Hormozi

emails.subject: Mozi Money: Proof > Promise
emails.body:
Words I like: "People don't trust you because you haven't done anything yet. In the real world, the proof comes before the pudding."

Mozi Money Minute

No matter how good your product is - a stranger won't believe you because you're biased. The easiest way to overcome that is overwhelming social proof. And yet - so few businesses do it. Here's an easy tactic to display tons of proof you already have but aren't showing.

If brick and mortar: Print out every 5-star review (or screenshot it) across all review sites online. Then frame them with $1 frames from the dollar store. Cover your lobby floor to ceiling in them.

If you are online, screenshot every 5-star on review sites + messages with customers + posts inside your communities. Then, add them to your sales page + presentations. Think *endless scroll*. Do it. It'll make you more money than providing no proof.

Proof > promise,

Alex Hormozi

emails.subject: The Mozi Money Minute: Middle Pricing
emails.body:
Perspective: When I was 20 I wanted to be a millionaire...Now that I'm a millionaire, I want to be 20."

It makes me realize that when I'm 40, I'd rather be 34 (my age) with nothing, just to be 34 (again).

A different frame of gratitude I thought I'd share.

Mozi Money Minute: Middle Pricing. If you sell a cheap thing and an expensive thing, and you want more people to take your most expensive thing, add a mid-tier third option.

Put it exactly in between your cheap and your expensive thing from a features perspective.

But, then price it only 10% cheaper than your most expensive option. This will skew more people to see the more expensive thing you sell as a deal.

If you even get 10% more people to take your more expensive thing, this email did its job.

Deploy & enjoy.

Alex Hormozi

emails.subject: Mozi Money Minute: Turn 7s into 9s
emails.body:
Perspective: "If you can be in a bad mood for no reason, you might as well be in a good mood for no reason."

Mozi Money Minute: Turn 7s to 9s.

Long ago, someone switched their prices from ending in 9s to ending in 7s.

And maybe, they got an increase in conversion. But those days are gone. We've never seen a difference in take rates.

People buy $99 and $97 at the same rate. They buy $999 & $997 at the same rate. Etc.

So change your 7s to 9s and make more money for the same work.

Same goes for adding .99 to your .00s.

It won't change take rates but you'll make more money.

For those of you in lower ticket higher volume businesses especially, this can easily add up to material cash.

Beyond that - stay rich.

Alex Hormozi

emails.subject: What i'm focused on right now + 1 instant money hack
emails.body:
What I'm working on right now media-wise:

- I made 6 videos in the last 2.5ish weeks that are deep business. You'll note that the views on them are somewhat staggered. The more conceptual ones with higher views, the more tactical with lower views. I don't see that as meaning tactical = less good. I just see that tactical applies to fewer businesses while all businesses gain value from a concept. Sharing these thoughts to provide insight for those of you making content.
- Looking at my best videos on my YT channel (best = most business-centric x views), many of them were keynote talks. So, I decided to make more of those in slide format. I presented them on my stage at my HQ to my team only (it's why the room sounds empty). After doing it, I don't think it was the slides per se, I think there's something about having a live audience that makes those talks *special*.
- So, I'm trying to figure out how to have a live audience. Ideally in person. When I figure it out, I'll let you know.

[Instant money hack] Something you can do right now to ROI this email:

CC Processing. A lot of businesses pay credit card processing fees of 3-4%. But they don't charge processing fees. If that's you, consider doing so. If you make 20% margins, and add 4% fees, you increase your profit by 20% (24% vs 20%). It won't affect your close rates, but it will affect your bottom line. Deploy and enjoy.

Just win,

Alex Hormozi

emails.subject: A thank you gift for your audience (for affiliates only)
emails.body:
It's Alex.

We don't have much time.

This week, I promised I was going to send you early access for your audience to something special.

Here it is. You can just copy paste the email.

Send this to your Audience

We were some of the most active participants in promoting Alex Hormozi's book event. So he just sent me this (as a THANK YOU) to give to you guys exclusively.

**See email from Alex below**

For the first time, I've just made Acquisition.com hats available to anyone who decides to share a couple of books.

There are only 30k hats available. If you would like one, this is the link to get one: shop.acquisition.com

For context: These hats are extraordinarily special for a couple of reasons.

- Whenever I start a new business, it is the first thing I always make. It makes the dream in my head feel more real. It's become a good omen of business success.

- These hats have been the MOST requested item people have been asking for in the last two years.

I have had countless people try and reach out to buy them. Not only that. People have come up to my team and made them offers for theirs.

Currently - only people who work at Acquisition.com get one.

You have to earn them. It's what makes them special and I never want to lose that.

And so - for anyone who wants to help the mission of making real business accessible to everyone. And is willing to buy some extra books to give out - to help a friend, family member, or another entrepreneur.

Then I would like to give you one of these Acquisition.com hats for free.

In my mind, you will have earned it - just like any of my team has.

So if you're interested, here is the link to take me up on this offer

Thank you in advance for supporting the mission.

Alex

PS - If you're reading this, it's because I asked for this to be sent to you.

The reason that matters -

If you don't like this email for any reason. The fault is mine. Not on the person who sent it.

I asked them as a favor. That way, you had early access and a unique opportunity to get something very few people will ever have.

What's more, if someone has given this email to you… Know this:

I can confidently say they have your best interests at heart because they have no financial incentive to share.

Whoever they are. They are one of the good ones. You are in good company.

emails.subject: SHHHHH It's a SURPRISE!!
emails.body:
Hey everyone! It's Leila.

Alex's birthday is today!

I'd love to surprise him with a flood of social media posts wishing him a happy birthday and good luck for the $100M Leads live event tomorrow as a surprise.

SURPRISE means it's a secret - Alex has no idea!

To give you an idea of what I was hoping to do…

For anyone who would like to get involved please:

-  Post a quick selfie or video with a @hormozi tag.
-  Wish Alex a happy birthday.
-  And wish him good luck! Or let him know you'll see him virtually tomorrow when he releases his brand-new book live!

I'd love to see your posts. And I encourage y'all to be creative.

I think so many of you have unique ideas and personalities it will make for such a fun compilation :)

(And if you would rather not, feel free to ignore this message.)

So again, to participate, please post something on social media, tag Alex @hormozi, and wish him a happy birthday for me.

I'd love to make this a birthday Alex won't forget.

Thank you all!

Looking forward to seeing you all tomorrow at the virtual event too.

Leila x

PS - In case you skipped the wall of text above lol

Alex's birthday is today! I'd love it if everyone could help surprise him and show some support for the book launch tomorrow.

Send him a quick Happy Birthday post @hormozi and wish him good luck.

And if you are showing up, let him know you'll be there to support him. Thank you.

PPS -  feel free to dress up like Alex (one feature makes the most sense, like a beard, hair or outfit).

Good news: I'm giving away a lot of stuff on Saturday. And one, is extremely valuable.

Bad news: I only have limited amounts. (only 6% of people who registered could get one).

Good news: If you're on live, you will probably be able to get one. And if you email your audience immediately after - they will too.

Alex.

PS -  This is ON TOP OF my 4-year secret project. I know. Lots of goodies. But…only for people who show up because I will a) run out b) only give them to people there live with me.

Mozination -

There are only 5 days to unlock 2 bonus chapters.

If you don't know I wrote two extra chapters to the $100M Leads book.

The good news: you can unlock them by bringing 10 friends with you to the free virtual event on the 19th of August.

Join the $100M Lead Games

Alex

Alex

Show up Saturday, August 19th, at 9 am PST/12 pm ET.

emails.subject: Revealed: My Whisper - Tease - Shout method
emails.body:
Mozination –

Only 11 days until $100M Leads.

When you get $100M Leads, you'll see exactly why (and how) I'm doing this countdown.

Let me show you the play.

It's called the Whisper -> Tease -> Shout Method. (I'm looking at it now on page 235).

The reason for mentioning it: I'm using it for this book launch.

So what you're seeing here (with these emails) is a real-life example you can come back to.

I'm a huge fan of walking the talk.

Because you're here and part of this community I wanted to let you know.

Here's some insider knowledge for you: so far with "whispers", we've added 200,000+ to the book launch waitlist.

If you saw my last email (Bigger than the Superbowl?), you'll have seen we've added an additional 140,000+ people since - with "teases".

Wait until you see the "shout".

Look at the image above again. The black hides what's to come…

To follow along, and see how it all pieces together, make sure to keep reading.

All will be revealed on the live announcement soon.

Sorry for teasing you like this. ;)

Alex

P.S. No sneak peek today.

Instead, If you're a founder or business doing $1- $10 million in EBITDA (profit) we'll help you accomplish your goals in 2-3 years vs 10.

Important question: if it did take less time to accomplish your goals, would that be okay with you?

If so, click below, apply on our website and let's build something great together.

Here's your application: Acquisition.com Application

I look forward to hearing from you.

emails.subject: Bonus Chapters
emails.body:
Mozination -

I wrote two extra chapters to the $100M Leads book. They won't be released with the book.

If you want them, read below.

My mission with Acquisition.com has been: to make real business education accessible to everyone.

I need your help to accomplish that.

Please send your friends/following to the free virtual book event.

It will be worth it for them.

We will be exporting the list of all live attendees so that we can give them the stuff I've been saving up for the last few years.

I say this knowing I will be attacked by everyone who does not make it. They won't think it's fair.

But I will make 'what I give away insane' because I want to be a man of my word. If I say 'it's worth it' I want that to mean something. So that next time I say 'you should come, it'll be worth it' you believe me. And the cost of that is the people who will be upset with me this time because they'll have missed it - and the goodies I'll give away.

Once you send 10 people to the event. And they show up live (it'll be worth it).

The way to get credit for it and get the two chapters is:

- Register as an affiliate here: acquisition.com/affiliate
- Email and share your custom link leading up to the 8/19 book event
- Make sure your 10 friends show up (or people from your audience)

People are calling the book launch event "the entrepreneurial event of the season" and "the Superbowl for entrepreneurs." Of course, I am the person calling it that. But it doesn't make it any less true.

Can't wait to see your reaction to the event.

Alex

PS - Go here to get your personal link: acquisition.com/affiliate

The top 10 referrers who invite the most guests, get a private round with me for your audience. Right now it takes 144 people to beat #10.

emails.subject: Btw… (I have a favor to ask)
emails.body:
Mozination -

Many people ask if there's a way they can "give back" to me - and normally - the answer is no - just win.

But today, I DO have something to ask...

$100M Leads (my next book) is coming out on the 19th of August.

And - word-of-mouth for a book can make a world of difference.

And so...if you'd like to help promote the $100M Leads Launch, we've created a cool way to share it (and get credit).

You can sign up and get a shareable link here.

Please email the link to your list and share to your audiences to tell them about the book launch (if it would serve your community).

As an added bonus:

1) The top 10 referrers who invite the most guests, get a private round with me for your audience. This way clicking your link actually benefits them - so you don't use up any goodwill. ;)

2) Anyone who gets 10 people or more to register gets 2 bonus chapters not being published. That way, even if you're not in the top ten you can still get something cool plus some extra goodies.

This means a lot to me. Hence the ask.

So if you've enjoyed 100M Offers... or any of the free content I've put out over the last two years, please share that value by inviting your audiences.

Thank you again,

Alex

PS - I'll love you either way. And I will continue to pump out as much content to help as I can.

PPS - For those who join, we'll add you to a separate list and send more backdoor details separately.

emails.subject: “I have nothing to sell you” is not true (please read)
emails.body:
Mozination – Over the past few weeks, I removed "I have nothing to sell you" from all my social media profiles and 114 YT videos.

Mozination -

Over the past few weeks, I removed "I have nothing to sell you" from all my social media profiles and 114 YT videos. And no, it's not because I have some course I'm selling.

Reason: I have a book for sale for .99. And even though Amazon takes .66 of the .99 (lol), it's still factually false.

You probably know me well enough by now - language precision matters a lot to me. So, since I technically have something "for sale" I will be removing it.

I want to be clear - my intention remains unchanged - I make the vast majority of my wealth from the companies we invest in - and I make this content to attract more 1% companies to invest in while giving to the 99% without an ask.

Good news: Without this technicality, it opens me up to provide some of the things y'all have been asking for: some hats and meetups for #mozination without worrying that I am 'defying' my core principle of giving.

My ask for Mozination: Please don't refer to me as someone who has "nothing to sell" since it's not technically true. I just want everyone on the same page.

What to expect now: Same as before - as much free content as I can muster to help you scale your businesses…and maybe a few hats ;)

Keep crushing. Appreciate you all,

Alex

PS - And of course -  if you've got a company doing over $3M-$100M in sales and want our help scaling it, go to: acquisition.com/apply.

It outlines the major forces I'm seeing in 2023 with AI, how it will disrupt each industry, and how I'm positioning our portfolio companies to profit from it.

I hope you get as much out of it as we put into it. Wanted to end the year with a banger video.

It just went live. Enjoy.

Alex

emails.subject: Today's the day
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

Today at 11am EST I'm going live with the AIOS workshop.

I've been finalizing the last section this morning. I keep adding things because I don't want to hold anything back.

This is going to be the most valuable free thing I've ever put out.

What you need: — 90 minutes — Add it to your calendar

Wednesday, February 25: 11am ET · 4pm GMT · 8am PT

I'm starting on time.

Liam

emails.subject: I read every single one of your questions
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

I've spent the last 48 hours reading through the questions you submitted when you registered.

1,846 of you wrote something.

And I keep seeing the same thing over and over:

"I'm not technical enough."
"I don't know where to start."
"I've tried tools but nothing sticks."
"I feel like I'm falling behind."

I hear you.

Here's what I want you to know before tomorrow.

I didn't start building my AI Operating System because I had some master plan. I started because I was drowning. 4 businesses, 50+ people, meetings stacking up, decisions falling through cracks, data scattered across 15 different platforms.

So I started building. One piece at a time. A data collector here. A daily brief there. A bot that captures voice notes and routes them to the right place.

Three weeks ago I locked myself in a room and went all in. And now I have a system that already runs huge chunks of my operation — and it genuinely scares me how good it is.

Tomorrow I'm showing you the whole thing. Not a highlight reel. The actual architecture, layer by layer.

And I built the workshop specifically around what YOU asked for:

— If you said "I'm not technical" → I'm showing you why that doesn't matter anymore.
— If you said "I don't know where to start" → I'm giving you the exact 7-day blueprint.
— If you said "I've tried ChatGPT and hit a wall" → I'm showing you why, and what to build instead.
— If you're already running a business and asked "how do I implement this safely?" → I'm walking through the 5-layer system running across my 4 companies right now.

I've never done anything like this before. This is the most I've ever shared publicly.

One ask: be there live. I'm doing demos that won't be in any replay.

Liam

emails.subject: Free workshop tomorrow — you need to see this
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

The future of business is here.

Over the past 3 weeks I've been locked in a villa in Cape Town building an AI Operating System around my entire business.

It generates my thumbnails, edits my videos, analyzes every meeting I take, builds my dev projects, runs my daily operations - all 4 companies...

50+ people, ~00/month.

I went from hours of daily admin to a 4-minute morning check-in.

And when you apply this to your business - you can genuinely run a one-person operation.

This is the biggest shift I've seen since I started. Tomorrow I'm breaking down the whole thing live.

Free workshop. The system, the architecture, and the framework to start building yours.

No coding required.

Wednesday, February 25
11am ET · 4pm GMT · 8am PT

Liam.

emails.subject: claude code is replacing 70% of my workload
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

I run my businesses from Telegram now.

A full AI operating system built on Claude Code — I'm calling it an AIOS — that briefs me daily about every call, Slack thread, and message across my entire business.

I reply from my phone and move on.

It's layers of modules that wrap around your business model:

Data from every source pulled into one queryable dashboard
Daily briefs that analyze everything that happened overnight
Meeting intelligence that recalls any conversation on demand
Inbox automation across iMessage, WhatsApp, and Gmail

Each module compounds on the last.

My goal: 60–70% of my workload handled by AI within 30 days.

Keep going,

Liam

P.S. I'm doing a live webinar showing how to set this up yourself.

emails.subject: we’re DONE building quietly
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

Last week was a big one for us at Morningside. We hosted our first-ever Morningside AI webinar - and over 2,000 people registered.

For the past three years, we've been quietly building. Working behind the scenes with organisations to become truly AI-first - from NBA and NRL teams to Fortune 500 companies.

We haven't spoken loudly about it. We've focused on delivering results.

But 2026 is different.

This is the year we start sharing what we've learned from implementing AI inside real organisations, where real numbers are on the line.

In this session, we broke down exactly how we helped a company reduce costs by $2.3M using AI, and how we measured that impact.

Most people talk about what AI could do.

But very few show how it actually moves financial metrics inside a business - and how to measure that impact properly.

Over the past year, we've refined a practical ROI modelling framework that we now use in every client engagement to pressure-test use cases before anything gets built.

And during the webinar, we gave that model away for free.

If you missed it, I've got something for you.

You can watch the full recording HERE - and at the end, you'll get access to the exact ROI template we use with our clients.

Enjoy - and keep an eye out for the next session. We've got a lot to share.

Liam.

Hey Justin - Liam here.

I am calling it.

From where I am standing, AGI has arrived - and its name is Claude.

The past two weeks I have been in Cape Town, locked in with some of the best people I know. Barely slept, barely eaten. And what I have been able to build in days would have taken my team months.

This feels exactly like when ChatGPT first hit the scene. The people who move now are going to be five steps ahead when everyone else catches up.

But there is too much to fit in one place. So I am running a free live webinar breaking down the complete system: the principles, the setup, and the starter template to get you moving immediately.

Let us get this,

Liam

emails.subject: the AAA model is bigger than i thought
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

I just wrapped up AfricAI - an event I led in Cape Town with agency owners who came through my program over the past few years.

It is honestly sick seeing people I mentored running real businesses across voice, automation, and custom software. I am not surprised they made it but I am blown away by what is now possible for anyone building in this space.

What we are shipping at Morningside right now, the engineering systems, the speed of delivery.. it has changed my entire outlook on where this model goes.

And after three years of data, the paths that actually work have never been clearer.

We are not guessing anymore, we are pushing forward - fast.

Keep going,

Liam

emails.subject: he made $75,000 in 3 months…
emails.body:
Hey Justin, Liam here.

Last week, Laurent Andriot closed a 75000 dollar deal with an insurance company as a full-time employee.

How did he do it?

Before I break this down, let us address what I hear constantly:

I don not have time for this.
I am working full-time.
Maybe when things slow down.

Here is the reality: Business does not care about your excuses.

You will not suddenly have more time. The AI industry is not slowing down. You will never feel completely ready.

Laurent understood this. He had the same constraints - full-time job, limited hours, life happening around him.

Here is how we helped him.

Using his unlimited coaching access, he booked 7 calls in three months - whenever he hit a blocker, he got answers.

He had a critical meeting coming up with the Senior VP and four IT representatives. We helped him structure his Statement of Work, nail his pricing, and handle technical objections to close the deal.

As a full-time employee, he also needed to position himself on LinkedIn without risking his job. We showed him how to build credibility the right way.

And because this was a regulated insurance company with strict compliance requirements, we helped him architect a solution for a bilingual system, select the right models for their Azure environment, and estimate scope accurately before presenting his proof of concept.

Could he have figured it out alone? Possibly.

But here is what I know: a lot of you have real goals. Financial freedom. A way out of the 9-5. Providing for your family. Building a better future.

And you have timelines for those goals.

So ask yourself:

Are you actually going to figure this out alone, or are you just telling yourself that?

If so, keep at it.

If not, check in with my team and discover if we can help you close that gap faster.

If it is not a fit, we will tell you straight.

Laurent did not leave it to chance. He used our resources - three years of data.

That is how he closed 75k in 90 days.

Liam.

P.S. Unlimited access means unlimited. You can book calls daily if you want with coaches who have seen every scenario over 3 years. They will tell you exactly what to do next.

emails.subject: the countdowns on
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

We are now just 72 hours away from the Morningside AI virtual event on 18 February at 3pm EST.

If you are registered but have not locked it into your calendar yet, now is the time.

In this live session, our team will walk through exactly how we helped a mid-market company save 2.3M using AI, including:

The identification process we use inside organisations
How we model ROI before building anything
The specific use cases actually driving measurable financial impact
Lessons from working across enterprise, professional sports teams, and mid-market companies

This will be practical, numbers-driven, and focused on implementation - not hype.

Looking forward to seeing you there.

Liam.

emails.subject: AI just had its iphone moment
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

In 2007, Steve Jobs held up a little rectangle and said this is going to change everything.

Most people saw a cool phone. What actually happened? It became a platform - and the people who built on top of it captured over 550 billion through the App Store.

That same structure just appeared in AI.

OpenClaw launched its own marketplace (ClawHub), 3000+ skills already live, and a barrier to entry that has collapsed to plain English.

The pattern is there: platform, marketplace, low barrier to entry.

This opportunity is not equally distributed though. Who wins and who gets eaten alive comes down to one thing most people are getting wrong right now.

Keep going,

Liam

emails.subject: stop using Claude Code like a chatbot
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

Most people treat Claude Code like ChatGPT - chatting back and forth, nudging outputs, wondering why nothing comes out right.

That is not how my team uses it.

We build workspaces - one folder with everything Claude Code needs to operate like a digital employee. Context about who you are, what you are working on, your current strategy, and your latest data.

Then instead of typing requests from scratch every time, we hand it reusable commands that run entire workflows on their own.

In my latest video, I am walking through the exact workspace template I train my staff on - including a live build where one command triggers deep research, pulls real-time YouTube data, and generates a full competitor analysis deck.

Keep going,

Liam

P.S. I also cover the 4 biggest mistakes beginners make that kill their outputs before they even start.

OpenClaw just had its App Store moment - and everyone is racing to build on it.

After playing with it myself, I am not changing a thing at Morningside AI.

Most people chasing this are about to learn a hard lesson about sand vs. rock.

Keep going,

Liam

P.S. If you are running an AI agency and feeling the pull - watch this before you pivot.

emails.subject: the secrets to scaling morningside
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

I am writing this from Cape Town, South Africa, where I am speaking at an AI Agency conference.

There is one question that keeps coming up over and over here...

How did Morningside scale from ~10k/month to 100k+ per month?

So as I am seeing more of you starting to hit that first 10-20k/month mark, I wanted to share exactly how we made that jump.

From 10k to 100k/month

For us, getting from 10k to 100k/month was actually fairly straightforward once we changed what we were selling.

We stopped chasing volume.

Instead of signing lots of 5-10k chatbot builds, we deliberately pulled back and focused on 3 clients who wanted to build real software with AI embedded into their core workflows.

In other words, we crossed AI services with proper software development (service stacked).

That one change dramatically increased our perceived value.

I still remember the first time we sent a 34k invoice in 2023 and realised: we do not just sell chatbots anymore, we sell outcomes.

That alone pushed us past 100k/month.

From 100k/month to 1M+/month: the second shift

A few years later, we realised something even more important.

95% of businesses do not just need help building AI. They need help figuring out what to build in the first place.

So we built a consulting layer on top of development.

My business partner Josh set up a dedicated consulting division at Morningside, hiring Nick, Eva, and Tara - consultants coming from the big 4 (Deloitte, EY etc.) in late 2024.

That is when we developed what we now call the AI Transformation Model.

How the AI Transformation Model works

We will go into a company and charge 30k-70k purely for identification work.

That includes:
Stakeholder interviews
Workflow and process analysis
Executive workshops
A final report outlining high-impact AI use cases

The key part?

In that report, we:
Prioritise the use cases
Estimate cost and ROI
Recommend whether Morningside should build them
Or hand them off to one of our Accelerator members as a client

For example, recently we identified 6 AI use cases that would save the client ~722k per year, with an estimated 420k build cost.

The takeaway for you

So, if you are at that 10k - 20k per month mark...

Stop positioning yourself as just a developer.
Start positioning yourself as a full-service AI transformation partner.

That is where the real money is.

And if this feels overwhelming - do not overthink it.

Start small:
Offer a 1k audit
Identify a few strong use cases
Deliver clear recommendations
Scale from there

Another interesting point: Accelerator members now have an average deal size of 28,000 - proof that it is not just us and these AOVs are achievable with the right framework.

I hope this was helpful.

Have a great weekend,
Liam.

emails.subject: we should talk
emails.body:
Hey Justin, Liam here.

I am writing this from a plane flying from New Zealand to South Africa.

I am heading to an event I am hosting alongside people like Nate Herk, Mark Kashef, Jannis Moore, and Taha El Harti - all well-known names in the AI space now.

What is wild is this:

I met most of these guys years ago inside the Accelerator.

I was on calls with Mark back in 2022 when he was just starting his agency.
I met Taha at our first Accelerator meetup in Amsterdam in 2023.
I remember early conversations with Jannis about his voice agency when it was still taking shape with another Accelerator member, Hendrik.

None of them had it fully figured out.

They just made a decision to commit early, stop waiting, and put themselves in the right room.

Fast forward to 2026, and we are standing on stage together.

That is why I keep saying this:

The room you choose matters.

We are already a month into the year.

So ask yourself - is 2026 another year of watching, or the year you actually build?

Back yourself.

I might be standing on stage with you next.

Liam

emails.subject: my #1 book recommendation
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

I have been revisiting a book recently that I think everyone trying to build something of their own should read at least once.

It is Losing My Virginity by Richard Branson.

What stood out to me was not the scale of what he built, it was how casually he stepped into uncertainty.

He did not know the record shop would work.
He did not know the music label would survive.
And when he started an airline, he openly admits he had no business doing it, except that he saw something broken and felt compelled to try.

That is the part people tend to miss.

Branson was not operating from certainty.

He was not waiting for validation or collecting guarantees before he moved. He was learning in motion, and adjusting fast when things did not work.

That is where most people hesitate. Failure feels personal, public, and permanent.

Branson did not avoid it. He failed publicly.

Virgin Group was on the brink of insolvency multiple times. And yet, none of that stopped him from taking the next swing.

Contrast that with how most people approach business today.

They are not short on information.
They are short on willingness to move without reassurance.

You want to know the outcome before you commit.
You want confidence before you act.
You want proof before you step in.

But business has never worked that way.

It did not for Branson. And it did not for us at Morningside either.

When I filmed my first YouTube video back in 2023, there was no proven AI agency playbook. No AI business model. No clear precedent for what we were building.

We were not following a category - we were creating one.

The AAA business model did not come from a case study or from watching endless videos. It was built through real execution, real client work, and a lot of uncomfortable decisions made without guarantees.

Fearlessness in business is not about being reckless. It is about accepting that uncertainty is the entry fee.

No one builds anything meaningful from a place of complete clarity. That only shows up after momentum, after reps, after real-world feedback.

The people who make progress are not fearless because they lack doubt. They are fearless because they refuse to let doubt dictate their decisions.

Just something worth sitting with and if you have not already, go read the book. Will give you a whole new perspective on how to approach business.

Talk soon,
Liam.

emails.subject: this founder crisis almost crushed me
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

There is a fork in the road most founders eventually hit:

Do I want to be a builder or seller?

On my recent trip to America, I ran straight into that crossroads.

I thought I had to choose.

Content burnout was weighing on me.

I kept questioning:

Have I said everything I had to say?
Am I just another voice in the noise now?

It triggered a full identity crisis.

So I did what comes naturally. I grabbed my camera and started filming.

That is when I realized something:

That fork in the road is a mirage.

The best entrepreneurs can go deep into the engineering trenches with their team, then pop back up to strategy and everything in between.

I started with technical skills. Built my first systems.

Then I drifted toward content and marketing because that is what the business needed.

But I do not want to be one-dimensional. Not just the content guy.

I want to be what I am calling a hybrid entrepreneur.

Someone who can go from idea to execution. Who can code alongside the devs, manage the technical teams, and translate that into content that actually connects. Someone who understands the product at the deepest level because they helped build it.

The connection between those skills? That is where the real spark happens.

As always - keep building,

Liam

emails.subject: you’re probably underpricing your projects
emails.body:
Hey Justin, Liam here.

Quick reality check.

Most people do not struggle to get interest in AI services.

They struggle to price them properly.

They second-guess the value.
They undercharge to get the deal.
They hope confidence shows up after the first few clients.

That is usually backwards.

A few weeks ago, Margot had a prospect ready to move on an AI automation.

Her original plan?
Charge 600 and call it a win.

After working through the offer with our team, she reframed the problem, clarified the outcome, and walked into the next call differently.

Same client.
Same scope.

She closed the deal for 3,000.

The key point is this:

It is about understanding what you are actually selling and learning how to communicate that clearly.

Liam.

ps: Margot did not wait until she felt ready.

She adjusted, took the call, and let the reps compound.

Think a few months out. What would change if your next deal was priced properly?

emails.subject: AI audits > websites
emails.body:
If there is one thing I have learned from 47+ client engagements over the past 3 years, it is this:

The AI audit is the new website.

In 2010, every business needed a website. The agencies who could build them printed money.

In 2026, every business needs an AI strategy. The agencies who can deliver one - through a structured, repeatable audit - will own the market.

Here is the problem: most AI audits are really just brainstorming sessions.

You hop on a call. You riff on AI tools. An hour later, the client says thanks, we will review and get back to you. Then they go dark. Or worse - they hire someone else.

What is missing is structure.

A repeatable process that:
Builds trust before you ever pitch a solution
Uncovers pain points the client did not even know they had
Produces a deliverable that makes them say when can we start?

We have spent 12 months refining this at Morningside. Running audits on everything from 20K quick wins to 150K+ enterprise projects.

I put together a quick overview of the 3-step framework we use - the same one that has closed six-figure deals.

This is how we have been doing it for over 12 months.

But all of this is a lot of hard work. What if you did not have to do it manually?

Well, my team and I have been working on something.

Can not say much yet - but if you have ever wanted to run audits without the 40-hour grind, keep an eye on your inbox.

- Liam

emails.subject: how we scoped a $500k project
emails.body:
Hey Justin,

Over the Christmas break, we closed the start of a project that will likely turn into a ~500k engagement.

And I want to give you some insight into how we did it.

My business partner Josh scoped the entire project - architecture, phases, timelines, risks, and staffing - without writing a single line of code.

Josh has not written a line of code in his life. He has next to no technical background.

I am calling that out for a reason.

Cause I hear people say:
I am not technical.
I do not have an engineering team.
I would not even know where to start.

So how did Josh do it?

Normally, a project like this goes straight to our engineers. This time, that was not an option. The team was offline, the client needed budget approval fast, and the window to move was small.

So Josh leaned into the same tooling and operating process we now use across Morningside, specifically Claude Code. Loaded with real context from Morningside: prior projects, engineering standards, delivery decisions, and internal documentation.

Here is the part that has changed the game for us - and why I am sharing this.

You no longer need to be technical, or have a full team of developers, to scope serious projects.

Tools like Claude Code make it possible to take real business context, real constraints, real goals and turn that into a clear, structured scope that you can actually move forward with.

That is exactly what Josh did here.

The key point is this:

Large AI projects are won or lost before a single line of code is written.

In this case, Josh worked from a small set of inputs: a recorded workflow walkthrough, example documents, and a short brief outlining success. From that, the scope was mapped clearly enough to move forward with confidence.

When our engineers came back online and scoped the project independently, their assessment matched roughly 85-90% of what had already been laid out.

That is not about cutting corners. It is about removing unnecessary bottlenecks.

The last piece - and this one matters more than most people think - is speed.

Because we moved quickly, the client was able to secure budget approval before year-end and move straight into the POC.

Most people assume the bottleneck in this space is capability.

In reality, it is hesitation.

Companies are moving. They are under pressure. And they are not waiting around for teams who need months to feel ready.

I wanted to share this because a lot of people assume there is a massive technical gap between where they are now and how agencies actually operate.

Most of the time, that gap is smaller than you think.

That is not theory. It is what I am seeing firsthand.

Liam.
