emails.subject: they all did this…
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

Yesterday I hosted a free webinar with over 700 people.

And I talked about one of the biggest mistakes I see people make when they enter the AI space.

Most jump straight to the end goal.

They want to build an AI software company.
They want to be a full AI transformation partner.
They want to teach, educate, and position themselves as experts.

Big ideas. Ambitious goals.

But time and time again, they all run into the same wall.

When you look at the most successful people inside the Accelerator, there is a clear pattern.

Mohan has built an AI software company scaling past 2M ARR.
Mert and Arda built an AI transformation firm doing 400k+ in the last 6 months.
Nate Herk went on to build one of the strongest personal brands in AI education.

But here is the part most people miss:

That is not where any of them started.

They all began in the same place - by building an AI agency first.

They learned the fundamentals by doing the work:
How the technology actually works
How to land and work with AI clients
How to market in a crowded AI landscape
How to build solutions that create real, measurable impact

Too many people try to walk before they can run.

If you are planning to get into AI this year, I strongly recommend following the same path:
Start by building solutions for real clients.
Solve real problems.
Create real change.

Talk soon,
Liam

emails.subject: “i’ll figure it out later”
emails.body:
Justin, going to be direct with you here.

My upcoming Masterclass is not for everyone.

If you are just curious about AI, or happy staying in I will figure it out later mode...

You can skip this.

But if you know AI is the opportunity you need to move on and you are stuck on how to actually make the transition...

This is exactly for you.

Most people I speak to are not lacking effort or ambition.

They are balancing jobs, families, maybe other businesses on the side. And they do not want to choose the wrong path and waste six months going nowhere.

I get it.

This Masterclass is to give you clarity on choosing the right path. I am talking actual paths, actual decisions, actual next steps.

Final call if you are serious about making a change in 2026.

Liam.

emails.subject: is this actually you?
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

I hear this all the time.

I know this AI path works for other people. I have seen it on podcasts. I have read the case studies.

But will it actually work for me?

Fair question.

In the upcoming Masterclass I am hosting, I will show you indisputable proof from two people I have helped on two completely different paths.

One started with no technical background. Did not know Python from a snake.

The other was hands-on but stuck tinkering with YouTube tutorials going absolutely nowhere.

Same outcome: Both made a clear and smooth career transition into AI without guessing what to do next.

In the Masterclass, I will be breaking down how you can do the same no matter your experience.

Here is what we will cover live:

The two proven AI career paths (and which one fits your situation)
How to transition without torching your current income
Real breakdowns (not just success stories but actual tactical moves)
The biggest mistakes people make (and how to avoid them)
A clear roadmap you can follow starting now

If you are serious about change, and want to understand how this can work for you, make sure you are there live.

Liam.

emails.subject: don’t be sexy (boring is better)
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

Since AI feels so new and disruptive, it is easy to assume everything has changed.

But when it comes to actually making money with AI, the truth is almost stupidly simple:

Pick one real business problem...

Build a system that solves it reliably.

That is it.

Your dentist website looks like it is stuck in 2005?

Fix it with AI in minutes.

Sell them the upgrade.

Client wants more sales calls?

Install an automated booking system.

Charge for the outcome.

Notice what you are not selling?

AI.

You are selling results.

Is it sexy? No.

Is it stable? Absolutely.

Every way to get rich with AI in 2026 looks the same:

AI is the toolset.

Rapid execution is the edge.

The fundamentals have not changed.

Boring, reliable outcomes win. Every time.

Keep going,

Liam

emails.subject: The "safe" path isn't safe anymore
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

I talk to professionals every week who tell me the same thing:

I know AI is the move. I just do not want to make the wrong one.

I get it. You have built something. You have responsibilities. You are not 22 anymore with nothing to lose.

But here is the truth:

The biggest risk in 2026 is not choosing the wrong AI path.

It is choosing no path at all.

No path does not mean things stay the same. It means you are hoping your industry does not get disrupted. Hoping your role does not get automated. Hoping you will be the exception.

That is not a strategy. That is a gamble.

The professionals who transitioned in 2025 understood this. They saw what was coming. But they did not leap blindly either.

They pulled off the smooth transition, moving into AI without torching what they had already built.

See you there.

Liam

emails.subject: we’ve got a call
emails.body:
Hey Justin, Liam here.

I will be direct.

January usually splits people into two groups.

There are those who use it to make decisions, set direction, and actually start moving toward something new.

And there are those who stay in I will do it tomorrow mode - consuming more content, waiting for the perfect moment, and telling themselves they will start later.

If you are in the second group, and building or scaling an AI business is not a priority for you right now, feel free to skip this email.

But if you are in the first group, my team has something for you.

We have just opened a limited number of spots for free discovery sessions heading into 2026.

This is not a sales pitch.

It is a focused conversation to understand your AI business goals for the year ahead and see whether we can help you either:

land your first AI clients, or
scale what you have already started.

If it is not a fit, we will tell you honestly.
If it is, we will help you map a clear path forward.

Looking forward to connecting,

Liam

PS: Not sure this could work for you?

Andres and Jana thought the same before joining Accelerator.

Five months later, they closed a 45k deal for their AI agency.

To be clear - they worked hard and started sprinting from day one. This takes effort.

But think about May 1st this year (five months from now).

What would it feel like to be closing deals like that - and actually changing the trajectory of your 2026?

emails.subject: our 3-year announcement
emails.body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

Today marks a special day for me...

Three years ago, I started Morningside AI.

Back then, it was just me in my room building basic chatbots and wondering whether any of this would actually turn into something real...

Fast forward to today and we are a 55+ person team across Morningside AI and the Accelerator, helping some of the world biggest companies actually deploy AI in real-world environments.

So to celebrate the milestone, I wanted to do something a little different...

Last week I messaged my team of engineers and coaches and asked them one question:

What is your one secret for building a successful AI Agency in 2026?

They came back with these 5 tips:

Tip 1 - You do not have to be a developer
The biggest myth in the AI space is that you have to be a developer to run an AI Agency. My business partner Josh runs operations at Morningside. He has never written a line of code in his life, yet he is helping run a world-class AI agency working with massive clients. Businesses care about the outcome, not the technical explanation.

Tip 2 - Think Bigger!
Most people think AI agency deals cap out at 5k, 10k, maybe 15k. Reality check: Our Accelerator members average 32k per deal. Morningside closed multiple 500k+ projects this year alone. If the leverage is big, the budgets are big.

Tip 3 - Development is only a fraction
About 30% of a project is building the system. About 70% is optimisation, adoption, and change-management. If AI projects fail, it is usually because this part gets ignored.

Tip 4 - Get out from behind that screen!
An online presence helps, but some of the biggest wins still happen in person. Three of our largest deals last year came from face-to-face relationships.

Tip 5 - You do not need a huge amount of capital
Josh used Claude Code to scope an entire multi-six-figure project. When one of our engineers reviewed it later, it was 95% aligned with their own scoping. A non-technical founder can pitch the solution, validate feasibility with AI, collect a deposit, then hire their first developer after the deal closes.

The blunt reality of starting an AI business:

Anyone can build an AI agency. But most people simply will not. They will over-research. They will tool-hop. They will try to figure everything out at once and burn out.

The opportunity is not slowing down. But it does reward the people who stop waiting and start moving.

Keep going,

Liam
